data.title: Wells Fargo’s ‘Core’ List: GOOGL, DIS, HD, WMT, and more!
data.description:
Wells Fargo analysts updated their “Core” list, “a favored list of industry-leading companies appropriate for long-term investment.”

These are blue chip, industry-leading, and high-quality stocks that could be used to build a well-diversified portfolio or to supplement an existing one, according to John Sheehan, equity sector analyst at Wells Fargo.

To put the list together, analysts focus on companies that have at least a mid-cap market cap and a substantial revenue base. Analysts also consider long-term revenue, earnings, common dividend growth rates, and projected future growth. In addition, they look at management’s level of experience, return on invested capital, stability of revenues and earnings, stock price volatility, balance sheet strength, debt rating, and other various valuation measures, Sheehan said.

Here is the Core list:

Within communication services

  • Alphabet (GOOGL) – Consensus long-term EPS growth estimate: 12%
  • Comcast (CMCSA) – Consensus long-term EPS growth estimate: 5%
  • The Walt Disney (DIS) – Consensus long-term EPS growth estimate: 16%
  • Omnicom Group (OMC) – Consensus long-term EPS growth estimate: 7%

data.title: FDA accepts AstraZeneca's sBLA for Imfinzi for bladder cancer
data.description:
• AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) said the FDA has accepted its supplemental Biologics License Application for Imfinzi for Priority Review for the treatment of muscle-invasive bladder cancer, or MIBC.
  • The agency is expected to issue a decision on the application during Q2 2025.
  • If approved, Imfinzi will be the first and only perioperative immunotherapy regimen in the curative-intent MIBC setting, according to a statement.
  • Imfinzi is already approved for the treatment of small cell lung cancer.

data.title: Court upholds ban on TikTok; rival social media stocks gain
data.description:
A federal court upheld the upcoming U.S. ban on social network TikTok (BDNCE), refusing to intervene after an appeal by the Chinese-owned service claiming the law was unconstitutional.

Stocks in rival social-media names reacted: Snapchat parent Snap (NYSE:SNAP) jumped up to 7%, while Facebook and Instagram's parent, Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), increased gains beyond 2%. Alphabet was also higher in midmorning: (NASDAQ:GOOG) +1%, (GOOGL) +1%. And conservative-focused video platform Rumble (RUM) had risen more than 20% Friday ahead of the court's ruling.

TikTok had sued the United States looking to stop a federal law -- the Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act, signed by President Biden in April -- calling for TikTok's divestment from Chinese parent ByteDance vs. imposing a ban in U.S. app stores by Jan. 19.

"We recognize that this decision has significant implications for TikTok and its users," concluded Judge Douglas Ginsburg of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit. "Unless TikTok executes a qualified divestiture by Jan. 19, 2025 — or the President grants a 90-day extension based upon progress towards a qualified divestiture ... its platform will effectively be unavailable in the United States, at least for a time. Consequently, TikTok’s millions of users will need to find alternative media of communication."

"That burden is attributable to the [People's Republic of China's] hybrid commercial threat to U.S. national security, not to the U.S. Government, which engaged with TikTok through a multi-year process in an effort to find an alternative solution," he went on.

"The First Amendment exists to protect free speech in the United States. Here the Government acted solely to protect that freedom from a foreign adversary nation and to limit that adversary’s ability to gather data on people in the United States."

With the ban set to take in effect in just over six weeks, TikTok (BDNCE) is now expected to appeal the decision to the U.S. Supreme Court.

And assuming the ban survives to Jan. 19, it's still unclear what happens in the upcoming second Trump administration. Trump famously pushed to ban the app during his first term as president, but warmed up to it particularly during his campaign for the 2024 election, and is expected to try and stop the ban from taking effect after he assumes the presidency Jan. 20.

data.title: Jobs numbers, on average, 'feel like' sustainable full-employment: Chicago Fed's Goolsbee
data.description:
• On average, the November jobs report "feels like" it's in the "sustainable full-employment space," Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said Friday at the bank's Annual Economic Outlook Symposium.
  • "We're chugging along," he added, noting that the jobs report appears to be showing that the labor market isn't getting better or worse.
  • The labor market is in better balance and a steadier state than it had been, Goolsbee explained.
  • Breaking news... check back for updates.

data.title: Fortrea falls after Baird cuts after sudden conference cancellations
data.description:
Fortrea (NASDAQ:FTRE) dropped 7% after the contract research organization was downgraded at Baird to neutral from outperform after the company suddenly cancelled from several conferences.

"Given our ongoing concerns around the sector, FTRE’s choppy history post spin, and lack of clarity on the abrupt communications course change, we cannot recommend an actionable investment (buy or sell)," Baird analyst Eric Coldwell wrote in a note on Friday.

Fortrea (FTRE) abruptly cancelled from two conferences this week and a planned non-deal-roadshow with Baird, according to Coldwell and traders told Seeking Alpha on Thursday.

“We’ve made a decision not to communicate until after the close of the quarter," Fortrea told Baird, which has a $25 price target on FTRE. "We have no further comments.”

Coldwell wrote that he was surprised that Fortrea (FTRE) shares surged 10% on Wednesday as investors found out that the company had cancelled conferences this week, with the market assuming that a takeover may be coming.

"While there are technically safe harbor situations available that might free FTRE from breaking the tax-fee nature of the spin, or perhaps the expected tax hit is manageable and a buyer might be willing to absorb it (?), the post-spin restrictions lift on July 1, 2025/ To us, this doesn’t feel like an ideal time to sell/LBO," Coldwell added.

Fortrea was spun off from Laboratory Corp. (LH) in July of last year.

The cancellations come after activist Corvex Management in late October disclosed a stake in Fortrea (FTRE). The company can fix its margin issues or pursue a potential buyout next year, Corvex's Keith Meister said in October at an activist conference.

The Corvex stake comes after fellow prominent activist investor Starboard Value first disclosed a stake in Fortrea (FTRE) last October. Starboard had a 9.4% stake in Fortrea as of the end of September, the company's third-biggest holder.

More on Fortrea Holdings

  • Fortrea: Some Green Shoots After A Dismal Period
  • Fortrea Holdings, Inc. (FTRE) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Fortrea Holdings Inc. 2024 Q3 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation
  • Fortrea shares soared Wednesday after reportedly canceling from conferences
  • Keith Meister's Corvex opens fresh stakes in Dollar Tree, APD, exits CAE: top Q3 trades

data.title: Golden Matrix Group reports 85% growth rate in Q3 revenue Y/Y
data.description:
• Golden Matrix Group (NASDAQ:GMGI) achieved $41M in consolidated revenue in Q3, 2024, an 85% growth rate year-over-year.
  • In Q3, 2024, year-to-date revenue grew by 55% to $105.3M and gross profit increased by 39% to $22.4M.
  • The company reports a 60% growth rate in shareholder equity and over $38.4M in cash reserves, exceeding its $17.5M in short-term debt.
  • Golden Matrix Group is nearing formal approval for entry into Brazil’s regulated market. Licenses for brands that meet the correct requirements are expected to be granted by the end of the year.
  • GMGI shares were up 4.7% on Friday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Novartis reports positive Phase 3b data for Fabhalta in PNH
data.description:
Novartis (NYSE:NVS) reported positive Phase 3b data from a study of its drug Fabhalta for the treatment of the rare blood disorder paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria, or PNH.

The study, called APPULSE-PNH, showed that oral Fabhalta, also known as iptacopan, improved the average hemoglobin level versus baseline in adults with PNH who were switched from anti-C5 therapies, according to a statement.

The Swiss drugmaker said the results add to the body of evidence showing the drug is safe and effective for adults with PNH.

Fabhalta was approved by the FDA in 2023 for the treatment of adults with PNH. It received accelerated approval in August 2024 for the reduction of proteinuria in adults with IgAN at risk of rapid disease progression.

data.title: Meyer Burger skyrockets after securing $39.5M bridge loan following key client's exit
data.description:
Meyer Burger Technology (OTCPK:MYBUF) +219% in European trading on Friday after the Swiss solar panel maker said it secured $39.48M in bridge financing, including an immediate $19.7M drawdown, to stabilize its embattled financial position.

The secured loan comes after the company's top client, D.E. Shaw Renewable Investments, terminated its master agreement with the company last month.

Meyer Burger (OTCPK:MYBUF) said it is in advanced talks with D.E. Shaw Renewable about a new offtake deal with updated terms, which it hopes to conclude later this month.

Meyer Burger (OTCPK:MYBUF) Executive Chairman Franz Richter said he saw interest from D.E. Shaw Renewable and IKEA operator Ingka in solar cells not made in China, especially after the election of Donald Trump as U.S. president, who is expected to raise tariffs on Chinese imports; Richter said renewed interest in the U.S. partly helped secure the bridge loan.

Richter said the bridge loan should fund sufficient liquidity for Meyer Burger (OTCPK:MYBUF) to reach agreements with bondholders and DESRI to reach a sustainable restructuring solution to stabilize its long-term finances.
